title: In conversation with Sam Altman
description:
(0:00) Welcoming Sam Altman to the show!

(2:28) What's next for OpenAI: GPT-5, open-source, reasoning, what an AI-powered iPhone competitor could look like, and more

(21:56) How advanced agents will change the way we interface with apps

(33:01) Fair use, creator rights, why OpenAI has stayed away from the music industry

(42:02) AI regulation, UBI in a post-AI world

(52:23) Sam breaks down how he was fired and re-hired, why he has no equity, dealmaking on behalf of OpenAI, and how he organizes the company

(1:05:33) Post-interview recap

(1:10:38) All-In Summit announcements, college protests

(1:19:06) Signs of innovation dying at Apple: iPad ad, Buffett sells 100M+ shares, what's next?

(1:29:41) Google unveils AlphaFold 3.0

analysis.summary: The All-In Podcast features Sam Altman, discussing OpenAI's future plans, AI regulation, and his personal experiences. The conversation also covers topics like fair use, creator rights, and the potential impact of advanced AI agents on app interfaces. Additionally, the hosts discuss Apple's decline in innovation and Google's unveiling of AlphaFold 3.0.

takeaways.text: Advanced AI agents will change app interfaces
OpenAI has stayed away from the music industry due to fair use concerns
AI regulation and UBI are crucial in a post-AI world
Sam Altman has no equity in OpenAI
AI-powered iPhone competitor is a potential future development

sentiment.notes: The conversation covers a wide range of topics, from the exciting future of AI to the concerns about fair use and creator rights. The hosts' sentiment is mixed, reflecting both the potential benefits and drawbacks of AI advancements.

notable_moments.description: Sam Altman discusses his equity in OpenAI
